note: First iteration using top-level claude -p sessions via harness-executor-tester.sh. Separate project copies to avoid contamination. Step 6+8 improvements from iteration 7 carried forward.

delta.notes: with_skill 3.3x pass rate (90.9% vs 27.3%). Both fixed the bug correctly. with_skill costs $2.55 vs $1.37 but produces: execution plan, full 5-step validation, trace, memory, git commit. delegates_to_subagent failed because task was correctly classified as Simple.

progress_across_iterations.note: Top-level sessions, separate projects, Step 6+8 validated

analyst_notes: STABLE HIGH PASS RATE: 90.9% for second consecutive iteration. The skill improvements from iteration 7 (mandatory full pipeline + artifact requirements) are holding.

TOP-LEVEL SESSIONS WORK: Using claude -p with harness-executor-tester.sh produces clean, reproducible tests. Separate project copies (hp-sleeper-test-with_skill, hp-sleeper-test-without_skill) prevent contamination.

delegates_to_subagent NOTE: This assertion is N/A for Simple tasks. The agent correctly classified the 2-line bug fix as Simple and directly edited — this is proper skill behavior. To truly test delegation, we need a Medium/Complex task (e.g., 'add a new API endpoint with iOS support').

COST COMPARISON: with_skill $2.55 vs without_skill $1.37 — the skill adds ~$1.18 overhead for the structured workflow (context loading, validation pipeline, trace/memory writing). This seems reasonable for the quality improvement.

BUG FIX ACCURACY: Both agents correctly identified the root cause (inverted gte/lte operators) and fixed it identically. The skill doesn't improve bug-fixing accuracy but ensures the fix is validated and documented.
